mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rapid industrial production growth triggers inflation fears and anticipated monetary tightening, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the predictive coefficient flips positive with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested sub-periods, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent forces markets to price in aggressive Fed tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong GDP growth and weak retail sales indicates inventory buildup and impending demand contraction, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, falling inflation combined with stable interest rates confirms disinflation without recession, driving strong positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative average 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in out-of-sample tests.
